Remember the guy who got a forehead tattoo for the Pumpfun bounty?

What do you think he's doing today with the $30k he received?

Option A: Logging off and using the money in real life to help his family.

Option B: Gambling on Polymarket by betting on the Indian Premier League (current P&L: -$744)

The team acknowledged the mistake (something rare in crypto) and fixed my account.

In situations like this, I realize how important it is to have an account with some reach to get a problem resolved quickly.

I also realized how difficult it can be for people without a public voice to be heard in similar situations.

I'm always available to act as a megaphone (after verifying the claims) if you have a problem with a specific platform and aren't able to get your voice heard.

My DMs are always open.

I just got banned from @trylimitless prediction market platform and accused of "wash trading."

The reality is that I simply had a profitable bot that made 1800$ after a lot of fine tuning.

Those were the accusations:

> "Wash trading": To do wash trading, you need to constantly buy and sell the same asset.
The bot NEVER SOLD A SINGLE POSITION and always held until expiry.

>"Point farming": I was not that high (290) in the leaderboard. I literally discovered today that there was an airdrop and claimed ~$670.
At least this was the good part.

I am happy to show all the proof of what I am saying since they didn't show any proof of their claims.

My mission here has always been to help people fight scams.

Unfortunately, I've realized that scammers are much better at attracting people who have just joined crypto with clickbait content like this.

The solution I found is to create clickbait content too (sometimes).

But instead of shilling shitcoins, rugs, or presales with shady teams, I'll try to help you avoid losing money.

If you already know me, I'm sorry you had to read a post like this today.

If you don't, I wanna give the most likely explanation for this "insider of the Spanish team"

Did we just have the first World Cup game completely rigged for money on Polymarket?

"Fishalive" made ~$9 million by betting on Spain not winning against Cape Verde.

> First trade: Bet $4.5M against Spain 8 minutes before the game began

> Everything came from a network of freshly funded Coinbase wallets with no previous activity

This is just a conspiracy theory, but it's really odd.

Some explain me this.

> Fresh wallet with zero history
> The only bet: Spain not winning against Cabo Verde

8.5 Million profit.

Was the game rigged? https://t.co/agvZKWc0GA

Insane.

After grinding with +1k predictions on Polymarket this wallet was up 34k.

Today he lost 1.6M on a single trade because Spain wasn't able to win against Cabo Verde https://t.co/hURJczBRnc
